seoTitle: How to Use Velo by Wix: Developer Mode Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to activate Velo developer mode on Wix, write basic JavaScript, and connect page elements to a database collection.
intro: Velo is Wix's full-stack development environment for adding custom code and database-driven behavior beyond what the standard drag-and-drop editor allows. This guide covers turning on developer mode, writing a simple JavaScript function for an interaction, and connecting front-end elements to a database collection.

steps.heading: Enable Developer Mode
steps.body: In your Wix editor, toggle Developer Mode from the upper navigation bar to unlock the code environment.

steps.heading: Explore the code panel
steps.body: Review the integrated code IDE and element tree layout to understand where custom scripts live.

steps.heading: Assign element IDs
steps.body: Use the properties sidebar to give your page elements unique ID handlers so code can reference them.

steps.heading: Write a basic function
steps.body: Add a simple JavaScript event function, such as animating a button on click, to see the code environment in action.

steps.heading: Set up a database collection
steps.body: Open the database manager to create a fresh collection for dynamic content.

steps.heading: Connect elements and test
steps.body: Bind your layout elements to the database fields, then use live preview to debug and confirm the interactions work.

faq.question: Do I need prior coding experience to use Velo?
faq.answer: Basic JavaScript knowledge helps, but Velo is designed to layer coding capabilities onto the existing visual editor rather than requiring you to build everything from code.

faq.question: Can I use Velo alongside the regular drag-and-drop editor?
faq.answer: Yes, Velo adds a code layer on top of your existing visual design rather than replacing it.

faq.question: What can I build with Velo that I can't with the standard editor?
faq.answer: Things like custom database-driven content, dynamic interactions, and tailored logic that go beyond built-in Wix apps.

faq.question: How do I debug my custom code?
faq.answer: Use the live preview environment to monitor debug console logs and catch errors before publishing.
